summary: All code_refs to stream_pacer.py verified at or near cited lines: defaults (min_remaining_audio=5.0, max_text_length=300), flush condition (generation_stopped and remaining_audio <= min_remaining_audio), first-sentence-immediate, docstring quote (exact), poll 0.2s while generating, sleep remaining_audio - min_remaining_audio. text_pacing default-off opt-in confirmed in stream_adapter.py. together-talk source covers streaming/interruption themes but the note attributes no specific number/quote to it (mechanism is code-sourced), so nothing to refute there.

claims.found: line 97: 'async def _send_task(self) -> None:' — the core pacing loop; SentenceStreamPacer class at line 19
line 141: 'first_sentence  # send first sentence immediately'; line 134 'if first_sentence or (...)'

claims.claim: flush only when generation stopped AND remaining_audio <= min_remaining_audio (default 5.0s)
claims.found: line 134-135: 'if first_sentence or (generation_stopped and remaining_audio <= self._options.min_remaining_audio)'; default min_remaining_audio: float = 5.0 (line 20)

claims.claim: each batch capped at max_text_length (default 300 chars)
claims.found: line 20: 'max_text_length: int = 300'; line 142 'sum(len(s) for s in batch) >= self._options.max_text_length'

claims.claim: docstring states intent (exact quote about buffering/waste/larger chunks/context)
claims.found: lines 22-24: 'It buffers sentences and decides when to flush based on remaining audio duration. This may reduce waste from interruptions and improve speech quality by sending larger chunks of text with more context.' — exact match

claims.claim: polls audio progress every 0.2s while generating, otherwise sleeps up to remaining_audio - min_remaining_audio
claims.found: line 159: 'wait_time = 0.2  # check more frequently when generation is in progress'; line 161 'wait_time = max(0.5, remaining_audio - self._options.min_remaining_audio)'

claims.claim: pacer is opt-in (text_pacing=True on StreamAdapter), default off
claims.found: stream_adapter.py:31 'text_pacing: SentenceStreamPacer | bool = False'; :43 'if text_pacing is True: self._stream_pacer = SentenceStreamPacer()'

claims.claim: Pipecat SimpleTextAggregator forwards each completed sentence immediately (divergence via [[TTS-03]])
claims.found: SimpleTextAggregator exists at pipecat/src/pipecat/utils/text/simple_text_aggregator.py:20; divergence claim is delegated to TTS-03, not asserted with a direct citation here
